title: Pessimistic lock: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE
prompt_md:
When you must read a row, think, then write — and conflicts are likely — you take a **pessimistic lock** so nobody else can touch the row until you commit. Select the `id` and `label` of the seat with `id` `3`, locking it with `FOR UPDATE`.

Any other transaction that tries to lock or update this row will **wait in line** until your transaction finishes.
starter_code: SELECT id, label FROM seats WHERE id = 3 ...;
hints: Append `FOR UPDATE` to the end of the SELECT to lock the matched rows.
reference_solution: SELECT id, label FROM seats WHERE id = 3 FOR UPDATE;
